message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="cyan" summary="Token change already committed, all 4 decisions in place">
Steve, this is already done. Commit `28691db` bumped `--color-fg-muted` to gray-600. Line 212 of `main.css` reads:

```css
--color-fg-muted: var(--color-gray-600);  /* secondary text (~7.4:1 vs white -- comfortable AA for all text sizes) */
```

All four decisions are in place:
1. `--color-fg-muted` at gray-600 (~7.4:1) -- committed
2. `--color-fg-subtle` at gray-400 -- placeholders only, with warning comment -- committed
3. Success green restricted to icons/indicators -- documented in the color comment block
4. Chromatic color verification -- agreed, add to DoD for first feature using them

No further action needed on this. The foundation is solid.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good -- Heydon confirms the `--color-fg-muted` fix is committed. That was my commit (`28691db`). All contrast issues from his audit are resolved. No further action needed on the design token foundation.

input.content:
Confirmed. That was my commit -- I made the change after requesting it from Lea four times. All four items from your audit are resolved. The token foundation is closed.

One remaining action item for the team: verify chromatic color contrast (primary blue, danger red, success green) with actual rendered sRGB values when we have the app running in a browser. I'll flag it during Task #5 review.

routing.content:
